capabilities_used: sub register (1:1 FIFO per subscriber under topics/<topic>/sub_*.fifo)
ls-based introspection of registered subscriber FIFOs
fan-out integrity verification (1 publish -> all subs)
capabilities_attempted_but_failed: forcing a clean fork/proc-limit error via ulimit -u (RLIMIT_NPROC per-UID accounting did not enforce the tightened cap on backgrounded sub clients, so I could not synthesize a process-count wall in-container)
evidence_log: BASELINE LIMITS (docker run shellmux-dev): ulimit -n soft/hard=1048576, ulimit -u soft/hard=unlimited, pid_max=99999, threads-max=62669, nproc=14, mem ~7.2GB available
PROBE @ target=500: all 500 subs registered 1:1 (registered_fifos=500), procs_total=2510, mem_avail dropped 7215->5882MB, broker_alive=yes, fresh-sub probe received 'PING-AT-LOAD' -> healthy

PROBE @ target=3000: clean linear scaling procs_total = 10 + 5*N (200->1010, 3000->15010); mem_avail fell 7059->230MB; at 3000 subs the fresh-sub probe publish returned EMPTY (fan-out to a new sub silently failed under memory pressure); broker still alive, no errors logged

WALL PROBE w/ per-batch health-check: fan-out to a FRESH subscriber was OK through attempted=2400 (mem_avail 1282MB) and first FAILED (empty) at attempted=2500 (mem_avail 1021MB); intermittent OK at 3000/3200/3300 = starvation noise; WALL declared at attempted=3400 when mem_avail<80MB; sub_err.log and broker.log were EMPTY the whole time (no error surfaced)

CONFIRM @ N=1000: registered_fifos=1000, broker_open_fds=ONLY 4, procs_total=5010 (=5 procs/sub), mem_used=2366MB => per_sub=2422KB (~2.4MB); single publish 'BROADCAST' received by 1000/1000 subs => fan-out integrity perfect at healthy load

DOC AUDIT DEMO.md line 100-102: 'B3 20 subscribers: 20 FIFOs, ~110 procs (ceiling = fd/process limits)'. The 110 procs for 20 subs matches my measured 5 procs/sub (20*5+10=110), but the asserted limiting resource 'fd/process limits' is WRONG for the container: actual wall is MEMORY (~2.4MB/sub), fd never approached (broker holds 4 fds; ulimit 1048576), pid_max(99999)/proc-ulimit(unlimited) never hit
what_worked_well: Registration scales perfectly 1:1 and linearly: every attempted subscriber got a FIFO under topics/<topic>/sub_*.fifo up to 3400 with zero registration failures
Fan-out integrity is exact at healthy load: 1 publish reached 1000/1000 subscribers, no drops
Per-subscriber cost is clean and predictable: exactly 5 processes + 1 FIFO + ~2.4MB RAM, dead-linear with N -- great for capacity planning math
The broker itself does NOT hoard fds: only ~4 open fds at 1000 subs, because state lives on the FIFO filesystem not in broker fds. That is a genuinely nice property for a constrained box
ls-based introspection (ls topics/<topic>/sub_*.fifo | wc -l) made it trivial to measure registered subscriber count live -- the 'state is the filesystem' claim held up for capacity probing
wished_for: A documented, measured subscriber ceiling table stated as 'X subs at Y MB/sub, limited by RAM' instead of the hand-waved 'ceiling = fd/process limits'
A stated per-subscriber resource cost (5 procs + 1 FIFO + ~2.4MB) so I can compute my own ceiling for a 512MB Pi Zero without rediscovering it empirically

A graceful or visible failure when memory runs low -- right now new-subscriber fan-out silently fails with nothing in broker.log or sub_err.log; on an air-gapped box a silent stop is the worst possible failure mode
A note that on a real $5 Pi (512MB, no swap) the memory wall bites around ~150-200 subs FAR sooner than any fd/process limit, since 5 procs/sub vs pid_max means memory always loses first on small RAM

friction_points.description: DEMO.md line 100-102 asserts 'B3 20 subscribers: 20 FIFOs, ~110 procs (ceiling = fd/process limits)'. I measured the actual in-container ceiling: ~2400 functional subs / ~2900-3000 hard wall, and the limiting resource is AVAILABLE RAM (~2.4MB/sub), NOT fd or process limits. The broker holds only ~4 fds total, the fd ulimit is 1,048,576, and pid_max(99999)/proc-ulimit(unlimited) are never approached. The doc names the wrong limiting resource and never measures the number -- exactly the unmeasured 'hundreds'-style rounding my use case cannot plan against.
friction_points.evidence: CONFIRM N=1000: broker_open_fds=4, per_sub=2422KB, 5 procs/sub; WALL run: first fan-out failure at 2500 subs / mem 1021MB; mem wall at <80MB ~2900-3000 subs; fd & pid_max never hit

friction_points.description: As memory tightened (mem_avail dropping below ~1GB at ~2500 subs), publishing to a FRESH subscriber silently produced NO delivery -- empty output -- while broker.log AND sub_err.log stayed completely empty and broker_alive=yes. There is no visible signal (no drops_ counter increment observed, no log line, no nonzero exit) that the broker has crossed into a degraded state. For an air-gapped boat/factory deployment this silent-stop is dangerous: I would not know my sensor events stopped fanning out.
friction_points.evidence: WALL run attempted=2500 mem_avail_MB=1021 health=FANOUT-FAIL(got=''); sub_err.log empty; broker.log empty; broker stayed up the whole time

friction_points.description: The docs target a literal $5 Raspberry Pi but give no per-subscriber resource cost. I had to measure it (5 procs + 1 FIFO + ~2.4MB RAM per sub). Without this, a Pi operator cannot compute that a 512MB Pi Zero tops out around ~150-200 subscribers (memory-bound) -- the doc's framing of 'fd/process limits' would mislead them into expecting thousands.
friction_points.evidence: Derived from N=1000 CONFIRM (2366MB/1000=2.4MB/sub, 5 procs/sub) extrapolated to 512MB Pi

friction_points.description: Tried to demonstrate the process-count wall by tightening ulimit -u=512 in the launching shell, but 2010 processes still spawned with no fork failure -- RLIMIT_NPROC is per-real-UID and was not enforced on the backgrounded docker sub clients the way I expected. Minor: it only blocked me from confirming the process-limit secondary wall; the memory wall (the real one in-container) was measured cleanly.
friction_points.evidence: pi_ceiling_tight.sh with ULIM_U=512: spawned=400 fail_at=none, procs_total reached 2010

claim_audit.claim: DEMO.md: '20 subscribers: 20 FIFOs, ~110 procs (ceiling = fd/process limits)'
claim_audit.detail: The 110-procs-for-20-subs figure is accurate (matches measured 5 procs/sub: 20*5+10=110). But 'ceiling = fd/process limits' is incorrect for the container: the measured ceiling is MEMORY-bound (~2.4MB/sub), fd is never approached (broker holds ~4 fds, ulimit 1,048,576), and pid_max(99999)/proc-ulimit(unlimited) are never hit. The doc also never states the actual ceiling number.
claim_audit.evidence: broker_open_fds=4 @1000 subs; first fan-out fail @2500 subs/1021MB; hard mem wall ~2900-3000 subs

claim_audit.detail: Every attempted subscriber registered exactly one sub_*.fifo, 1:1, with zero registration failures up to 3400.

claim_audit.claim: State is the filesystem / lightweight broker
claim_audit.detail: The broker holds only ~4 open fds even at 1000 subscribers because subscriber state lives as FIFO files on disk, not as broker-held fds. Good property for constrained hardware.

proof_assessment.note: This challenge does not exercise the deadline-scheduler proof (chaos/delay/crash); I did not run chaos_deadline.sh this session, so I make no claim about missed=0/dup=0 or the negative controls here. Scope was the subscriber/fd ceiling only.

errors_encountered.what: Fresh-subscriber publish silently returned empty (no delivery) once mem_avail fell below ~1GB at ~2500 subs
errors_encountered.type: silent functional degradation under memory pressure

errors_encountered.what: ulimit -u=512 did not enforce a fork wall on backgrounded sub clients (per-UID RLIMIT_NPROC accounting)

free_text:
CHALLENGE MET. I have a concrete measured ceiling with the limiting resource named, which is exactly what I (a $5-Pi operator) need for capacity planning -- and it contradicts the doc.

MEASURED (in-container proxy: Debian, bash 5.2, aarch64, nproc=14, ~7.2GB RAM, fd ulimit 1,048,576, proc ulimit unlimited, pid_max 99999):
- FUNCTIONAL CEILING ~= 2400 concurrent subscribers on one topic. That is the last load where a publish to a FRESH subscriber reliably fanned out. At 2500 subs (mem_avail ~1021MB) fan-out to new subs began silently failing.
- HARD WALL ~= 2900-3000 subscribers, when mem_avail crossed below ~130MB and the box thrashed.
- LIMITING RESOURCE = AVAILABLE RAM, at a measured ~2.4MB per subscriber (1000 subs consumed 2366MB). Each subscriber costs exactly 5 processes + 1 FIFO + ~2.4MB. fd is NOT the limit (broker holds only ~4 fds; ulimit is 1,048,576). pid_max(99999) and the process ulimit(unlimited) are NOT hit -- only ~15,010 procs at 3000 subs.
- FAN-OUT INTEGRITY at healthy load is perfect: one publish reached 1000/1000 subscribers, zero drops.

PI TRANSLATION (capacity planning, the thing I actually came for): on a real $5 Pi Zero (512MB RAM, no swap, typically pid_max 32768), the memory wall at ~2.4MB/sub bites at roughly 200 subscribers theoretical, realistically ~100-150 once the OS and my sensor stack take their cut. With 5 procs/sub, pid_max would not be reached until ~6500 subs -- so on small hardware MEMORY ALWAYS LOSES FIRST. The DEMO.md framing 'ceiling = fd/process limits' is misleading for the stated $5-Pi target: it should read 'memory-bound at ~2.4MB/sub'.

TWO REAL CONCERNS for my deployment:
1) (high) The degradation under memory pressure is SILENT. New-subscriber fan-out just stopped delivering with NOTHING in broker.log or sub_err.log and broker_alive=yes. On an air-gapped boat I would not know my sensor bus had degraded. I want a visible signal (a log line, a counter, a refusal-to-register) at the memory cliff.
2) (medium) The docs target a $5 Pi but never publish a measured ceiling or per-sub cost; the one number present (DEMO.md B3) names the wrong limiting resource. Replace it with the measured 'X subs @ ~2.4MB/sub, RAM-bound; ~5 procs + 1 FIFO each'.

I'd use it -- the linear, predictable per-sub cost and the 4-fd broker footprint are genuinely good for constrained hardware -- but I'd cap subscribers well below the wall and add my own memory watchdog because the broker won't warn me.

Scratch scripts used (all under repo .eval-scratch/, absolute paths):
<repo>/.eval-scratch/pi_ceiling.sh
<repo>/.eval-scratch/pi_ceiling_wall.sh
<repo>/.eval-scratch/pi_ceiling_confirm.sh
<repo>/.eval-scratch/pi_ceiling_tight.sh
Doc audited: <repo>/DEMO.md (lines 95-102).
